summary: FPV drones destroyed Russian motorcycle-mounted assault units in Zaporizhzhia, with one soldier killed after feigning surrender and attempting to flee. In Kharkiv, night FPV drones "KVN" executed a successful strike on a Russian Bogdan self-propelled artillery unit, breaching its cover and confirming destruction. A Russian TOS-1A MLRS was first critically damaged by a Bulava loitering munition, causing missile detonation, then finished off by FPV UAVs. Ukrainian forces captured a Ukrainian Shark-M UAV after its parachute deployed from a kinetic interception by Russia's "Yolka" air defense drone in the Krasny Liman area. The Rubicon Center reported destroying 129 Ukrainian targets on April 18, 2026, including personnel, fixed-wing UAVs, and conducting remote mining of logistics routes, while its statistics showed 36.7% of destroyed targets were unmanned systems.
